title: Viburnum ichangense
taxonomy: Viburnum ichangense is classified within the Adoxaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the elderberry or mosquito plant family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. The genus Viburnum is a group of flowering plants within the Adoxaceae family, and Viburnum ichangense is one of the species within this genus. The plant is native to China and is a member of the order Dipsacales. Viburnum ichangense is a deciduous shrub species that is commonly cultivated for its ornamental value and attractive flowers.

intro: Viburnum ichangense, also known as Changhai or Ichang viburnum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and the Himalayas. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, nausea, and respiratory issues. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, such as viburnin, isovitexin, and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its health benefits. In traditional Chinese medicine, Viburnum ichangense is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to promote immune function, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
